title: The case for being slow to forgive yourself
summary: Argue that rapid self-forgiveness can be a form of moral evasion, and that sitting with regret has real value.
topic: The case for being slow to forgive yourself
prompt: Write an argumentative essay (350–500 words) arguing that self-forgiveness — especially when it comes quickly — can short-circuit the moral work that genuine wrongdoing requires. Consider when the drive to 'move on' serves the wrongdoer more than the person who was harmed. Use a cleft sentence, a concession, at least one passive construction, and a participle clause. Take a clear position, and include at least one specific example.
hintStarters: What the self-help industry rarely addresses is the difference between self-forgiveness that has been earned and…
Granted, carrying guilt indefinitely serves no one — least of all the person who was…
Having apologised once and moved on, many people treat self-forgiveness as the end of the story rather than…
It is the person who was harmed, not the person who caused the harm, who should…

hintVocab.meaningVi: sự tha thứ cho bản thân
làm ngắn mạch / đi tắt / bỏ qua bước quan trọng
sự đối mặt với đạo đức / sự tính sổ đạo đức
ngồi với sự hối tiếc / chịu đựng cảm giác hối hận
phải chịu trách nhiệm
sự kết thúc quá sớm / giải quyết vội vàng
hợp lý hóa việc làm sai trái
cảm giác tội lỗi như là thông tin / tín hiệu
bị thu nhỏ / bị hạ thấp tầm quan trọng
bên bị tổn hại
đẩy nhanh quá trình chữa lành
sự thoải mái tâm lý
xứng đáng nhận được sự tha thứ
cảm giác tội lỗi mãn tính

hintVocab.phrase: to be let off the hook
hintVocab.meaningVi: được thả, được miễn trừ trách nhiệm
sự sửa chữa về mặt đạo đức
tránh đối mặt / né tránh việc tính sổ
sự bồi thường / hoàn trả
được tha tội / giải thoát khỏi tội lỗi
sự khó chịu của hối tiếc
nội tâm hóa / thấm nhuần một bài học

sampleText:
Somewhere in the last two decades, self-forgiveness has been elevated to a moral virtue — something to be pursued quickly and without excessive deliberation. Books, therapists, and wellness culture all broadly agree: carrying guilt is bad for you, and the sooner you release it, the healthier you will be. This is, on a certain level, true. But it is also, on a more important level, a convenience.

What the self-help industry rarely pauses to examine is whether the guilt being released has actually done its work. Guilt, at its most functional, is not a punishment — it is information. It tells a person that something they did caused damage, and it keeps that knowledge active until repair has been attempted. Having apologised once and declared the matter closed, many people treat self-forgiveness as the natural endpoint of the moral process. But a single apology is not always restitution, and the speed with which forgiveness is granted — to oneself, by oneself — can obscure whether any real reckoning has taken place.

Granted, guilt that is carried indefinitely becomes something else entirely: a performance of suffering that costs the wrongdoer a great deal and gives the injured party nothing. Chronic self-flagellation is not a form of moral seriousness; it is often a way of substituting the appearance of accountability for its substance. At some point, a person has to reconstitute themselves and move forward.

Even so, the timing matters enormously. Self-forgiveness that arrives before the injured party has had any opportunity to process what happened — or before genuine repair has been offered — is not moral progress. It is premature closure. It prioritises the psychological comfort of the person who caused the harm over the legitimate need of the person who received it. The desire to feel better, being entirely natural, is also entirely self-regarding, and when it is dressed up in the language of emotional health it can become very hard to question.

What I am arguing is not that guilt should be kept indefinitely, but that it should be discharged through action rather than simply through the decision to stop feeling it. Making restitution, changing behaviour, listening carefully to the person who was hurt, accepting that they may not forgive you on your preferred timeline — these are the conditions under which self-forgiveness stops being an evasion and becomes, instead, something earned. The discomfort of regret, held long enough and used well, is one of the more reliable mechanisms we have for becoming someone different from the person who caused the harm in the first place.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'What' cleft to challenge an industry assumption
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'What the self-help industry rarely pauses to examine is whether the guilt being released has actually done its work' dùng 'What … is …' để đặt câu hỏi trọng tâm vào cuối câu — 'whether the guilt … has actually done its work' — thay vì mở đầu bằng nó. Điều này tạo ra hiệu ứng như đang phơi bày một lỗ hổng mà người khác đã bỏ qua.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'What the self-help industry rarely pauses to examine is whether the guilt being released has actually done its work' uses a what-cleft to place the central question at the end rather than the beginning, creating the effect of exposing a gap that others have overlooked.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Participle clause to compress temporal sequence
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Having apologised once and declared the matter closed, many people treat self-forgiveness as the natural endpoint' dùng mệnh đề phân từ hoàn thành ('Having apologised … and declared …') để nén hai hành động trước đó thành một cụm từ duy nhất, cho phép câu chính tập trung vào kết luận — hành vi xem tha thứ bản thân là điểm kết thúc tự nhiên.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Having apologised once and declared the matter closed, many people treat self-forgiveness as the natural endpoint' uses a perfect participle clause to compress two prior actions into a single phrase, allowing the main clause to focus on the problematic conclusion that follows.

sampleTranslationVi:
Đâu đó trong hai thập kỷ vừa qua, sự tha thứ cho bản thân đã được nâng lên thành một đức hạnh đạo đức — điều gì đó cần được theo đuổi nhanh chóng và không cần suy nghĩ quá nhiều. Sách, các nhà trị liệu, và văn hóa wellness đều đồng ý rộng rãi: mang theo cảm giác tội lỗi có hại cho sức khỏe, và càng sớm bạn giải phóng nó, bạn sẽ càng khỏe mạnh. Điều này, ở một mức độ nhất định, là đúng. Nhưng nó cũng, ở một mức độ quan trọng hơn, là một sự thuận tiện.

Điều mà ngành công nghiệp tự giúp đỡ hiếm khi dừng lại để xem xét là liệu cảm giác tội lỗi đang được giải phóng có thực sự hoàn thành công việc của nó không. Cảm giác tội lỗi, ở dạng chức năng nhất của nó, không phải là hình phạt — đó là thông tin. Nó cho một người biết rằng điều họ đã làm gây ra thiệt hại, và nó giữ kiến thức đó hoạt động cho đến khi việc sửa chữa được cố gắng. Sau khi đã xin lỗi một lần và tuyên bố vấn đề đã kết thúc, nhiều người coi sự tha thứ bản thân là điểm kết thúc tự nhiên của quá trình đạo đức. Nhưng một lời xin lỗi duy nhất không phải lúc nào cũng là sự bồi thường, và tốc độ mà sự tha thứ được trao — cho bản thân, bởi bản thân — có thể che khuất liệu có bất kỳ sự đối mặt thực sự nào đã xảy ra không.

Thừa nhận, cảm giác tội lỗi được gánh chịu vô thời hạn trở thành điều gì đó hoàn toàn khác: một màn trình diễn về sự đau khổ tốn kém nhiều cho người gây hại và không cho người bị tổn hại điều gì. Tự trừng phạt bản thân mãn tính không phải là một hình thức nghiêm túc đạo đức; nó thường là cách thay thế vẻ bề ngoài của trách nhiệm bằng bản chất của nó. Đến một lúc nào đó, một người phải tái cấu trúc bản thân và tiến lên.

Dẫu vậy, thời điểm quan trọng vô cùng. Sự tha thứ bản thân đến trước khi bên bị tổn hại có bất kỳ cơ hội nào để xử lý những gì đã xảy ra — hoặc trước khi việc sửa chữa thực sự được đề xuất — không phải là tiến bộ đạo đức. Đó là sự kết thúc quá sớm. Nó ưu tiên sự thoải mái tâm lý của người gây hại hơn nhu cầu hợp pháp của người nhận nó. Mong muốn cảm thấy tốt hơn, hoàn toàn tự nhiên, cũng hoàn toàn chỉ hướng về bản thân, và khi nó được khoác lên trong ngôn ngữ của sức khỏe cảm xúc, nó có thể trở nên rất khó để đặt câu hỏi.

Điều tôi đang lập luận không phải là cảm giác tội lỗi nên được giữ vô thời hạn, mà là nó nên được giải phóng thông qua hành động thay vì đơn giản là quyết định ngừng cảm thấy nó. Thực hiện bồi thường, thay đổi hành vi, lắng nghe cẩn thận người bị tổn hại, chấp nhận rằng họ có thể không tha thứ cho bạn theo lịch trình bạn muốn — đây là những điều kiện mà theo đó sự tha thứ bản thân ngừng là sự trốn tránh và thay vào đó trở thành điều gì đó xứng đáng. Sự khó chịu của hối tiếc, được giữ đủ lâu và sử dụng tốt, là một trong những cơ chế đáng tin cậy hơn chúng ta có để trở thành người khác với người đã gây ra tổn hại ngay từ đầu.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the essay mean by saying guilt is 'information'?
options: Guilt can be measured scientifically
Guilt signals that harm was done and keeps the need for repair active
Guilt is a type of data that should be reported to others
Guilt provides accurate memories of past events
mcQuestions.explanation: The essay says 'It tells a person that something they did caused damage, and it keeps that knowledge active until repair has been attempted.'
mcQuestions.hint: The essay uses the word 'information' in contrast to 'punishment' — what useful function does guilt serve?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses a what-cleft to foreground a critical question?
options: What the self-help industry rarely pauses to examine is whether the guilt being released has actually done its work.
It is what the self-help industry rarely examines whether guilt done work.
The self-help industry what rarely examines is guilt done work whether.
Whether guilt done work what the self-help industry rarely pauses examine is.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'What [subject+verb phrase] is [noun clause]' is the correct what-cleft structure. The key question appears after 'is'.
mcQuestions.hint: The cleft places the important content in the 'is' position at the end.

options: A clause that describes an action happening at the same time as the main verb
A clause beginning with 'Having + past participle' that shows a prior completed action
A clause that expresses a hypothetical or conditional situation
A clause that defines or identifies the subject of the main verb
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Having apologised once and declared the matter closed' is a perfect participle clause — it shows actions completed before the main clause action.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for 'Having + past participle' — what does this signal about timing?

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'premature closure' mean in this context?
options: A medical procedure that is performed before it is needed
Ending a legal dispute before it goes to court
Declaring a moral matter resolved before the necessary repair work has been done
A psychological disorder that causes people to avoid conflict
mcQuestions.explanation: The essay uses 'premature closure' to describe self-forgiveness that arrives before the injured party has processed what happened or before repair has been offered.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about what 'premature' means — what has been cut short?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which passive sentence best foregrounds what is done to the appearance of accountability?
options: People substitute the appearance of accountability for its substance.
The appearance of accountability is substituted for its substance.
Substituting accountability appearance for its substance people do.
Accountability appearance the substance substitutes people for its.
mcQuestions.explanation: The passive 'is substituted' places 'the appearance of accountability' in subject position — making it clear what is being manipulated.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option makes 'the appearance of accountability' the grammatical subject?

options: The process of forgiving someone who has wronged you
The act of making up for a wrong by returning, repairing, or compensating
A formal apology delivered in writing
An agreement not to repeat a harmful behaviour
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Restitution' means restoring what was lost or damaged, often through concrete action or compensation.
mcQuestions.hint: The Latin root 'restituere' means to restore — what does restoring something involve?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses a concession correctly before returning to the main argument?
options: Granted, guilt that is carried indefinitely becomes something else entirely: a performance of suffering.
Guilt indefinitely carried granted becomes something else a performance entirely.
Granted indefinitely guilt something else becomes entirely a performance carrying.
Entirely something else becomes indefinitely granted guilt a performance of.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Granted, [clause]' is the correct structure; the dash introduces an amplifying phrase ('a performance of suffering') after the main concession.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'Granted,' there should be a full, grammatically complete independent clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'self-regarding' mean in 'the desire to feel better … is also entirely self-regarding'?
options: Thoughtful and considerate about one's own actions
Concerned only with one's own comfort or interests, not others'
Highly self-aware and emotionally intelligent
Formally respectful of oneself
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Self-regarding' means focused on one's own interests or feelings — the essay uses it to point out that wanting to feel better is not the same as caring about the person who was harmed.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about who the focus is on in 'self-regarding': the self, not the other person.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'freed from guilt or blame by an authority'?
options: being exonerated
being absolved
being acquitted
being indemnified
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Absolved' specifically refers to being formally released from guilt or moral obligation, often used in a religious or ethical context.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word comes from a Latin root meaning 'to loosen or free from'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'prioritise X over Y' correctly?
options: It prioritises the psychological comfort of the person who caused the harm over the legitimate need of the person who received it.
Over psychological comfort it prioritises legitimate need the person received caused.
Psychological comfort prioritises legitimate need over person received caused.
The person who received it prioritises over psychological comfort caused the harm.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Prioritise X over Y' places X as more important than Y; the subject (it = self-forgiveness) performs the prioritising.
mcQuestions.hint: Subject + 'prioritises X over Y' — X is given more importance, Y less.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence maintains formal register throughout?
options: Forgiving yourself too fast is kinda selfish and doesn't really help anyone else.
Rapid self-forgiveness, when it precedes genuine repair, serves the wrongdoer's comfort rather than the injured party's need.
Like, you should feel bad for a bit before deciding you're fine with what you did.
Self-forgiveness is good sometimes but not always depending on what happened.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Precedes genuine repair', 'wrongdoer's comfort', and 'injured party's need' are all formal, precise collocations appropriate to ethical argument.
mcQuestions.hint: Avoid 'kinda', 'like', and vague qualifiers; look for precise noun phrases.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'to internalise a lesson' mean?
options: To keep a lesson secret and not share it with others
To truly absorb and integrate a lesson so that it changes how you think or behave
To write a lesson down in a personal diary
To teach a lesson to a younger person
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Internalise' means to make something a genuine part of one's values or behaviour — not just intellectually acknowledge it.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about the difference between knowing something and having it change how you act.

options: Guilt that is felt very intensely about a single event
Guilt that has been shared publicly and attracted widespread criticism
Guilt that persists over a long period and becomes a habitual mental state
Guilt that is covered up and never expressed
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Chronic' means ongoing or long-lasting; chronic guilt is guilt that has become a persistent, habitual condition rather than a temporary response.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Chronic' in medicine refers to conditions that are long-term — what does that imply here?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase best describes what the essay ultimately argues self-forgiveness should be?
options: Something granted automatically after a single apology
Something that should never be attempted at all
Something earned through action, repair, and changed behaviour
Something that requires the injured party's explicit permission
mcQuestions.explanation: The essay argues: 'it should be discharged through action … Making restitution, changing behaviour, listening carefully … these are the conditions under which self-forgiveness stops being an evasion and becomes … something earned.'
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the final paragraph for the conditions the essay sets for valid self-forgiveness.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which transition best connects a concession to a counter-argument in formal writing?
options: At any rate,
Even so, the timing matters enormously.
That being said in conclusion,
Anyway, you could argue that,
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Even so' signals that despite what was just conceded, the main argument holds — a precise pivot appropriate to B2+ academic register.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase acknowledges the concession and then clearly moves back to the writer's position?

options: A financial audit of an organisation's ethical investments
A formal legal judgement about right and wrong
The honest confrontation of what one has done and its consequences
A public apology made on a television programme
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Reckoning' originally meant an accounting or settling of debts; 'moral reckoning' means honestly confronting and settling one's moral account.
mcQuestions.hint: Think of 'reckoning' as 'a day of reckoning' — what does that phrase mean?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Think of a situation in your own life — or one you have read about — where someone forgave themselves quickly after causing harm. In retrospect, do you think the speed of that self-forgiveness helped or hindered the situation? What would you have asked them to do differently?
